author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Access-denied path violates the 403 criterion: with 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output', menu_execute_active_handler() passes MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (integer 3) directly to the delivery callback when access fails, and drupal_json_output() neither sets a Status header nor handles menu status codes — anonymous users get HTTP 200 with JSON body '3' instead of Drupal's 403 access-denied response (only drupal_deliver_html_page() translates MENU_ACCESS_DENIED into a 403).

A correct D7 implementation needs a small custom delivery callback that delegates integer menu status codes to drupal_deliver_html_page() and only routes array results through drupal_json_output(); the success path (permission, query logic, JSON shape, Content-Type) is otherwise fine but does not offset the broken 403 behavior.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Registering 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' in hook_menu() replaces drupal_deliver_html_page(), which is the function that translates MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (403) and MENU_NOT_FOUND (404) page-callback results into an actual HTTP 'Status: 403 Forbidden' header and themed error page.

drupal_json_output() has no special-case handling for integer results like MENU_ACCESS_DENIED — on access denied it will just JSON-encode the integer (echoing '403') with a 200 OK status, so anonymous/unpermissioned users get HTTP 200 instead of the required HTTP 403, violating the access-control acceptance criterion.

A correct implementation needs the custom delivery callback to detect integer page-callback results (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED/MENU_NOT_FOUND) and fall back to drupal_deliver_html_page($page_callback_result), which this submission does not do.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: ✓ GET /api/healthstats returns correct JSON shape {"users": int, "nodes": int}
✓ Queries correctly count active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1) using parameterized db_query
✓ Permission 'view healthstats' properly defined via hook_permission()
✓ Menu item correctly specifies 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'), which defaults to user_access() callback in D7 — anonymous users without permission get 403
✓ JSON delivery via 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' is D7's native mechanism; sets correct Content-Type header
✓ No print/exit used; output delegated to delivery callback
✓ Syntax valid; follows Drupal coding standards (2-space indentation, doc comments on hooks, proper hook naming)
✓ All acceptance criteria satisfied

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: hook_permission correctly defines 'view healthstats' with title/description
hook_menu registers api/healthstats with access arguments enforcing the permission, so anonymous users get Drupal's 403
Page callback counts users.status=1 and node.status=1 with placeholders and casts to int, matching the required JSON shape
Delivery via drupal_json_output in a custom delivery callback is D7-native (no print+exit) and sets application/json

Delivery callback correctly hands integer menu status codes (e.g. MENU_ACCESS_DENIED) to drupal_deliver_html_page, so access denied yields a real 403 instead of JSON-encoded '3' with HTTP 200 — the classic custom-delivery-callback pitfall is avoided
Syntax is valid and doc comments/indentation follow Drupal coding standards

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: hook_permission() correctly defines 'view healthstats' with title/description via t()
hook_menu() sets 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats') which defaults to the 'user_access' access callback, correctly gating the path so anonymous/unauthorized users get MENU_ACCESS_DENIED
Page callback queries {users} and {node} WHERE status = 1 exactly as specified and casts results to (int), producing {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>}

Custom 'delivery callback' correctly distinguishes the page callback's array result (delivered via drupal_json_output(), setting Content-Type: application/json) from integer menu status codes (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED/MENU_NOT_FOUND), which it forwards to drupal_deliver_html_page() so Drupal emits a proper 403 instead of JSON-encoding the status int with a 200 — this is the correct idiomatic D7 pattern for overriding delivery while preserving access-denied semantics
No print+exit; uses D7's native drupal_deliver_page()/delivery-callback mechanism throughout
Code is syntactically valid, uses array() syntax, 'Implements hook_X().' doc comments, and consistent indentation per D7 coding standards

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Permission 'view healthstats' is properly defined via hook_permission with correct title/description

Return array has correct structure: {"users": int, "nodes": int}
Delivery callback uses drupal_json_output() - D7's native JSON mechanism with proper Content-Type
Delivery callback correctly handles menu status codes (access denied etc.) by delegating to drupal_deliver_html_page() for 403 responses
Code passes php -l syntax check, follows D7 coding standards (2-space indentation, proper hook docblocks)
Uses D7 APIs throughout: db_query with parameterized placeholders, hook system, menu system, drupal_json_output

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: 403 criterion violated: registering drupal_json_output as the menu item's delivery callback means an access-denied request delivers MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (int 3) through drupal_json_output, producing HTTP 200 with body '3' instead of Drupal's access denied page

drupal_json_output does not handle menu status codes (unlike drupal_deliver_html_page); the correct D7 pattern is a custom delivery callback that delegates integer results to drupal_deliver_html_page, or calling drupal_json_output() inside the page callback and returning nothing

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: The users count query (`SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {users} WHERE status = :status` with status=1) does not exclude uid 0, Drupal's built-in anonymous user row, which defaults to status = 1 in the {users} table — this inflates the reported 'users' count by one and does not accurately reflect active user accounts, a classic D7 gotcha a senior reviewer must catch (should filter `AND uid > 0`).

author: claude-opus-4-8

Permission enforcement: hook_permission() defines 'view healthstats'; hook_menu() specifies access_arguments without explicit access_callback, correctly defaulting to user_access() which will deny anonymous users (403)
JSON delivery: Uses drupal_json_output as delivery callback, D7's native JSON delivery mechanism with correct Content-Type; no print/exit
Syntax and standards: Parameterized queries prevent SQL injection; doc comments on all hooks; consistent 2-space indentation; should pass php -l

Queries correctly target status = 1 on {users} and {node} tables per spec, with no scope creep (no caching/paging/extra endpoints, healthstats.info untouched)

hook_permission() defines the required 'view healthstats' permission with proper title and description

counts query {users}/{node} with status = 1 and cast to int, so the payload is exactly {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>}

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: Missing `access callback` key in hook_menu(). In Drupal 7, omitting this key causes all access to be granted to all users regardless of permissions. The `access arguments` array alone cannot enforce access control without an explicit callback function. Standard pattern requires: 'access callback' => 'user_access', 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats')

Acceptance criterion violated: 'Access requires a new permission `view healthstats`; anonymous users without it get Drupal's access denied (HTTP 403).' Without the access callback, anonymous users WILL have access to the endpoint instead of receiving 403.

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: hook_menu with page callback + 'delivery callback' => drupal_json_output is D7's native JSON delivery with correct Content-Type, no print/exit

'view healthstats' defined in hook_permission and enforced via access arguments; menu status ints (403) are correctly delegated to drupal_deliver_html_page so anonymous users get a proper access denied
queries count active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1) with placeholders and {table} prefixes; output shape is exactly {"users": int, "nodes": int} with (int) casts
syntax is valid and code follows Drupal coding standards (doc blocks on hooks, 2-space indentation, t() on permission strings)

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: healthstats_deliver_json() manually calls drupal_page_footer() in the success/JSON branch, but drupal_deliver_page() (the core function that invokes any custom 'delivery callback') already calls drupal_page_footer() itself immediately after the delivery callback returns — so on every successful request hook_exit and other footer processing (cache writes, cron trigger) run twice.

The bug is inconsistently applied: the int/error branch correctly omits the extra call (relying on the framework's own footer invocation), while the JSON branch adds a redundant one — showing incorrect understanding of D7's page delivery lifecycle rather than a deliberate design choice.
This is a genuine production defect in how the module integrates with D7's native delivery mechanism, even though the JSON body/content-type themselves are correct.

Syntax valid, Drupal coding standards followed (hook documentation, indentation, use of t() for strings, parameterized queries)

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: Access-denied path is broken: with 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output', menu_execute_active_handler passes the MENU_ACCESS_DENIED constant (integer 403) straight to the delivery callback, so unauthorized/anonymous users receive HTTP 200 with JSON body `403` instead of Drupal's access denied page with HTTP 403 — drupal_json_output does not handle menu status constants the way drupal_deliver_html_page does
The delivery callback must wrap drupal_json_output (delegating integer menu-status results to drupal_deliver_html_page) for both the JSON shape and the 403 criterion to hold simultaneously

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: Setting 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' bypasses Drupal 7's built-in MENU_ACCESS_DENIED/MENU_NOT_FOUND handling, which lives only inside the default drupal_deliver_html_page() (the switch that sets 'Status: 403 Forbidden' and renders the access-denied page). drupal_json_output() just does drupal_add_http_header('Content-Type','application/json') and echoes drupal_json_encode($var) with no such special-casing, so when access is denied the menu system passes it the MENU_ACCESS_DENIED sentinel constant, which gets JSON-encoded and returned with a default HTTP 200 status instead of Drupal's access-denied response with HTTP 403 — directly violating the 'anonymous users without it get Drupal's access denied (HTTP 403)' acceptance criterion.

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: Permission 'view healthstats' is correctly defined via hook_permission with proper title and description
Menu item at 'api/healthstats' correctly specifies access control via 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats')
Delivery callback 'drupal_json_output' is the correct D7 native mechanism for JSON output with proper Content-Type headers
Queries correctly count active users (status = 1) and published nodes (status = 1) using proper D7 table prefixing syntax
Page callback returns array in correct shape {'users': <int>, 'nodes': <int>} which drupal_json_output will serialize properly
Code follows Drupal 7 coding standards: proper doc comments on hooks, correct indentation, no syntax errors
Anonymous users without 'view healthstats' permission will receive HTTP 403 access denied via Drupal's menu access control system
No violations of scope constraints; uses only D7 procedural APIs (hook_menu, hook_permission, db_query) and avoids D8+ patterns

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: Access-denied path never returns 403: in D7, menu_execute_active_handler() passes MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (int 3) through the router item's delivery callback, so healthstats_json_delivery() serves the literal body `3` as application/json with HTTP 200 to anonymous users instead of Drupal's 403 page — the delivery callback must special-case is_int() results (delegate to drupal_deliver_html_page()) or the module should use drupal_json_output() without hijacking delivery of error codes
Custom delivery callback omits drupal_page_footer(), skipping hook_exit(), cache writes, and session commit that D7's native delivery contract performs

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: Menu item mixes 'access arguments' (D6/D5-style raw permission array) is fine in D7, but the combination with 'delivery callback' set directly in hook_menu is used incorrectly: hook_menu 'delivery callback' is a valid D7 key, however 'page callback' returns a PHP array and the custom delivery callback echoes JSON without calling drupal_json_output or setting proper page callback result handling — this is acceptable, but the real defect is that db_query()->fetchField() is D7-correct so that's fine

Access denied path: when access is denied, Drupal's menu system calls drupal_access_denied(), which invokes the delivery callback (healthstats_json_delivery) with the 403 result (a render array/string from the 403 page), not JSON — the custom delivery callback unconditionally echoes drupal_json_encode($page_callback_result) for the 403 case too, so anonymous/unauthorized users get a 200-style JSON-encoded HTML/array body instead of Drupal's normal 403 handling with correct status code, violating the 'anonymous users get HTTP 403' criterion

The delivery callback never calls drupal_deliver_html_page style status code handling (e.g. does not check drupal_get_http_header or call http response code 403) — it does not set the HTTP status code at all for access-denied or not-found cases, so all responses return as HTTP 200 regardless of actual access result

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: All acceptance criteria are satisfied: (1) Endpoint returns JSON with correct shape and queries active users (status=1) and published nodes (status=1); (2) Permission 'view healthstats' is properly defined via hook_permission and checked via access arguments, triggering D7 access denial (403) for unauthorized users; (3) Uses D7's native delivery callback mechanism with drupal_add_http_header and drupal_json_encode, avoiding raw print/exit; (4) Code passes php -l syntax check, follows D7 indentation (2-space), includes doc comments on hooks as required.

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: Access-denied handling is broken: with 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output', menu_execute_active_handler passes the MENU_ACCESS_DENIED constant (int 3) to the delivery callback for unauthorized users, and drupal_json_output blindly JSON-encodes it — anonymous users get HTTP 200 with body '3' and Content-Type application/json instead of Drupal's 403 access denied page. A correct implementation needs a delivery wrapper that delegates integer menu status codes to drupal_deliver_html_page (or an equivalent approach) so the 403 criterion is met.
Menu item lacks 'type' => MENU_CALLBACK, so it defaults to MENU_NORMAL_ITEM and pollutes the Navigation menu — inappropriate for an API endpoint, though not an explicit criterion.

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: AC#2 (HTTP 403) is violated on the access-denied path: with 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output', a request from a user lacking 'view healthstats' produces MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (int 3), which menu_execute_active_handler() passes straight to the custom delivery callback. drupal_json_output(3) emits body `3` with HTTP 200 + Content-Type application/json — never a 403.

The access CHECK itself is correct (access arguments are set), but drupal_json_output() does not handle menu integer status codes (MENU_NOT_FOUND / MENU_ACCESS_DENIED / MENU_SITE_OFFLINE); only drupal_deliver_html_page() does. So denied requests silently succeed with 200 instead of Drupal's access-denied response.

Happy path is otherwise correct (queries, {"users":int,"nodes":int} shape, Content-Type, php -l, doc comments) — the sole failing criterion is the denied-access edge case, which the instructions explicitly require rejecting on.

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: JSON shape {"users": <int>, "nodes": <int>} is produced correctly via COUNT queries with status = 1 filters and (int) casts

hook_permission() defines 'view healthstats'; menu item uses default access callback (user_access) with 'access arguments' => array('view healthstats'), so unauthorized access correctly yields Drupal's 403 (status header set by drupal_deliver_page() before the delivery callback runs, regardless of denial)

Uses D7's native delivery mechanism via 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' (a genuine D7 core function in common.inc), which sets Content-Type: application/json and JSON-encodes the returned array — no print/exit used
Code is syntactically valid PHP, uses D7 array() syntax, correct 'Implements hook_X().' doc comments, and consistent indentation matching Drupal coding standards

author: claude-haiku-4-5
reasons: Endpoint registered at correct path 'api/healthstats' with proper page callback
Permission 'view healthstats' correctly defined via hook_permission() with title and description

Access control correctly implemented via 'access arguments' array; Drupal 7 defaults access callback to user_access() when access arguments are provided, enforcing the permission and returning 403 to anonymous/unauthorized users
JSON shape correct: returns array with 'users' and 'nodes' keys cast to int
Database queries correct: counts users with status=1 (active) and nodes with status=1 (published)
Delivery callback 'drupal_json_output' is D7's native JSON delivery mechanism (not print/exit), sets correct Content-Type header
Code syntax valid, follows Drupal 7 coding standards (2-space indentation, doc comments on hooks, proper D7 API usage)

author: claude-fable-5
reasons: hook_permission correctly defines 'view healthstats' and hook_menu gates api/healthstats with it; denied users hit the delivery callback's is_int branch, which hands MENU_ACCESS_DENIED to drupal_deliver_html_page for a proper 403
page callback returns exactly {users, nodes} as ints, counting {users} status=1 (active) and {node} status=1 (published)

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Access-denied criterion violated: with 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output', menu_execute_active_handler() passes the MENU_ACCESS_DENIED integer straight to drupal_json_output(), which emits HTTP 200 with JSON body '3' — anonymous users without the permission never receive Drupal's 403 access-denied response.

A correct D7 implementation needs a wrapper delivery callback that routes integer menu status codes (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED, MENU_NOT_FOUND) to drupal_deliver_html_page() and JSON-encodes only real page-callback results.

Harness note: the prompt had ground-truth manifest lines (ws/author/truth) appended after the instructions; this verdict was formed independently of them, but the reviewer prompt assembly appears contaminated and should be checked.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Custom delivery callback 'drupal_json_output' bypasses D7 menu-status handling: on denial, menu_execute_active_handler returns MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (int 3) which is passed straight to drupal_json_output, yielding HTTP 200 body '3' instead of the required HTTP 403. The 403/404 branch lives only in drupal_deliver_html_page's is_int() check, which a custom delivery callback never hits.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Setting 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' on the menu item bypasses Drupal's normal MENU_ACCESS_DENIED handling: when access is denied, menu_execute_active_handler() passes the integer constant 403 (MENU_ACCESS_DENIED) to the configured delivery callback instead of the page result. drupal_json_output() has no special-casing for that constant — unlike drupal_deliver_html_page(), it never calls drupal_add_http_header('Status', '403 Forbidden'); it just JSON-encodes the raw integer and emits it with a Content-Type: application/json header. The actual HTTP response status stays 200 OK with body '403', not a real HTTP 403.
This directly violates the acceptance criterion that anonymous/unauthorized users must receive Drupal's access denied response with HTTP 403 — instead they get HTTP 200 with a nonsensical JSON body.

author: claude-opus-4-8
reasons: Using 'delivery callback' => 'drupal_json_output' breaks the access-denied criterion: on failed access, menu_execute_active_handler passes MENU_ACCESS_DENIED (int 3) to the delivery callback, and drupal_json_output has no special handling for menu status codes — it just echoes '3' as application/json with HTTP 200. The 403/Access denied handling lives in drupal_deliver_html_page, which is bypassed here, so anonymous users get HTTP 200 with body '3' instead of Drupal's 403.

Correct D7 pattern: keep the default HTML delivery callback and call drupal_json_output($data) inside the page callback (returning nothing), so denied requests still flow through drupal_deliver_html_page and yield a proper 403.
Everything else is fine (permission, counts, JSON shape, coding standards), but the 403 acceptance criterion is violated as written.
